answer:
**In *Andreas*, the poem opens by invoking God's sovereign distribution of fates to the apostles ("Their lots were laid out as the Lord Himself, / Heaven’s High King, handed them their fates"),[^1] establishing divine providence as foundational. The core "intervention" moment follows Matthew's prayer in Mermedonian captivity: God speaks directly from heaven, promising rescue ("I shall not leave you lost in this torment... I will send Andrew to save you"), then commands Andrew by the sea ("You must go now... to free your kinsman").[^2] This personal divine speech launches the narrative, portraying God as capable of audible, relational intervention: hearing prayers, issuing specific commands across distances, providing miraculous aid (e.g., disguised angels as sailors), killing foes unseen, opening locked doors by Spirit-touch, and overriding natural laws—emphasizing a covenantal God who acts dynamically in history to save his elect amid human peril.**

**In *Manusmṛti* Book 1, the foundational creation unfolds as the Self-Existent One (Svayambhū) emerges from primordial darkness ("This universe existed as darkness... as if wholly asleep"; he "appeared with creative power, dispelling the darkness"), manifests as the eternal soul of beings, then—desiring progeny from his body—creates waters via contemplation, casts his seed (becoming a golden egg), births Brahmā within it, dwells a year, and splits it to form heaven/earth, atmosphere, directions, mind, ego, senses, elements, gods, varnas, time, and all beings.[^3][^4] This requires a god capable of solitary, self-generated cosmogony: unmanifest-to-manifest transition, tapas-powered emanation from subtle particles of self/mind, hierarchical ordering (e.g., varnas from body parts), cyclical waking/sleeping of worlds, and embedding dharma/actions eternally into creation—stressing an impersonal, all-encompassing cosmic architect sustaining order through inherent qualities and recurrence.**

*The theologies diverge sharply: *Andreas* needs a intimately providential God who micromanages crises with voice/commands/miracles for personal salvation (heroic, historical agency amid chaos); *Manusmṛti* needs a contemplative progenitor whose self-splitting/dividing crafts stable cosmic strata/duties (detached, structural perpetuity via tapas/elements).*

[^1]: *Andreas*, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-868413).

[^2]: *Andreas*, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-bdbda7); [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-4d1e78).

[^3]: *Manusmṛti*, [paragraph](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-61df11); [paragraph](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-93cef0); [paragraph](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-0eca16); [paragraph](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-54fad8); [paragraph](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-153021).

[^4]: *Manusmṛti*, [paragraph](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-a030b1); [paragraph](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-cd6713).
